name: Debbi's German Chocolate Cake
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup unsalted butter, at room temperature
4 1/4 cups shredded sweetened coconut, lightly toasted
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour three 9-inch round cake pans; tap out excess.
2. Prepare the cake: Melt half the chopped chocolate with 1/2 cup water in a microwave-safe bowl or saucepan. Combine flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt in a bowl. Beat butter and sugar until smooth and creamy. Add vanilla, egg yolks, and mashed potatoes; beat until well blended. Stir in melted chocolate.
3. Alternate beating in the flour mixture with buttermilk (beginning and ending with dry ingredients) for 45 seconds after each addition. Fold in beaten egg whites until no streaks remain.
4. Pour batter into prepared pans; divide equally. Bake for 20-25 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in centers comes out with moist crumbs. Cool cakes on wire racks for 10 minutes, then turn out to cool completely.
5. Prepare the frosting: Combine sugar, butter, evaporated milk, egg yolks, and vanilla in a large saucepan; cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until butter is melted, sugar dissolved, and mixture turns golden (12 minutes). Let stand until lukewarm and spreadable. Stir in remaining pecans and coconut.
6. Place one cake layer on serving plate; spread with frosting. Repeat with remaining layers. Frost sides of the cake with remaining frosting. Decorate with chopped pecans and toasted coconut.
